task: Deploy render_report.py and run Mesh Bioplastics Starter tier competitive intelligence test end-to-end

2_docxtpl_installed.import_test: from docxtpl import DocxTemplate; print('docxtpl OK')

3_render_report_py_deployed.details: Accepts --input and --output arguments, reads JSON payload, renders using DocxTemplate, saves to output/ directory

6_axis_labels_determined_by_claude.y_axis_label: Integrated AI Automation vs. Standalone Measurement
6_axis_labels_determined_by_claude.axis_rationale: Specific to the AI-powered injection moulding market
6_axis_labels_determined_by_claude.reasoning: Claude identified that positioning differences in this market are defined by: (1) how much companies innovate in bioplastic materials vs optimise existing processes, and (2) whether AI is embedded in the system vs offered as add-on measurement

7_mesh_bioplastics_scores.positioning: High on both axes — leading in Material Innovation AND providing Integrated AI Automation

8_rjg_scores.positioning: Moderate innovation; strong in standalone measurement technology
8_rjg_scores.perception_gap: Claims standalone technology leadership but lacks integrated AI

9_kistler_scores.positioning: Low material innovation focus; established in measurement systems
9_kistler_scores.perception_gap: Positioned as measurement authority; weak on integrated AI and material science

10_synventive_scores.positioning: Moderate on both axes; strong thermal flow control specialist
10_synventive_scores.perception_gap: Known for hot runner expertise; positioning as full-stack AI/material provider is emerging

11_perception_gap_detected.gap_description: All three competitors claim to be 'solution providers' but actual integrated capabilities vary. RJG and Synventive market themselves as full-stack but lack cohesive AI+material integration that Mesh delivers natively. Kistler remains measurement-focused with weaker material science narrative.
11_perception_gap_detected.market_implication: Mesh Bioplastics has clear differentiation: the only player combining bioplastic innovation with embedded AI at the point of production (injection moulding). Competitors are adding AI as add-ons, not foundational architecture.

opportunities.position: High AI Automation + Low Material Innovation (upper-left quadrant)
opportunities.description: Market gap for companies offering AI optimization for traditional plastics — Mesh could serve adjacent markets without material science overhead
opportunities.target: High-volume injection moulders wanting AI without reformulation complexity

opportunities.position: High Material Innovation + Low AI Integration (lower-right quadrant)
opportunities.description: Bioplastic innovators who lack process optimization — potential partnership or acquisition target for Mesh
opportunities.target: Research-stage bioplastic companies needing manufacturing scale

opportunities.description: Market leadership position — limited direct competition; strong first-mover advantage in integrated AI+bioplastic space
opportunities.target: Healthcare, automotive, and regulated manufacturing sectors

13_positioning_matrix_generated.content: 2D scatter plot showing Mesh Bioplastics (78, 85) dominating upper-right quadrant, with RJG (45, 72), Kistler (38, 68), and Synventive (52, 75) clustered in lower-left, illustrating Mesh's clear differentiation

15_report_pushed_to_github.git_commit: Mesh Bioplastics CI Starter test report

executive_summary.market: AI-powered injection moulding process optimization + sustainable bioplastics
executive_summary.key_finding: Mesh is the only player combining bioplastic innovation with native embedded AI (VariControl) in injection moulding. Competitors are adding AI as add-ons or maintain narrow specializations.
executive_summary.investment_perspective: Clear market leadership position in emerging segment with high barriers to entry (requires both material science AND manufacturing AI expertise)

dimension_1.name: Material Innovation vs. Process Control
dimension_1.definition: How much does the company prioritize bioplastic/material innovation vs. optimizing existing manufacturing processes

competitors.synventive: 52/100 — Hot runner design innovation; weak bioplastics

dimension_2.name: Integrated AI Automation vs. Standalone Measurement
dimension_2.definition: Is AI embedded natively in the production system, or offered as a separate measurement/monitoring add-on

competitors.rjg: 72/100 — Strong measurement; AI as secondary feature
competitors.synventive: 75/100 — Hot runner + emerging AI; not fully integrated

api_usage.reason_downgrade: Cost optimization; task required only structured competitive analysis, not deep reasoning
